label: Line 5: Protecting Great Lakes Drinking Water

comments.text: every single day, we’re treating nearly 200 million gallons of sewage and pumping it back clean to Lake Erie, the drinking-water source for more than 10 million people.

if you’re a customer of ours, the time it takes for water you flush to circle back to Lake Erie cleanly after treatment varies thanks to a lot of factors in the local and regional system. but the treatment process itself, once sewage reaches our plant, takes just about 24 hours.

I just secured over $5.6 million to support northern Illinoisans’ access to clean water. This funding will help replace lead pipes, upgrade water infrastructure, & bring clean Lake Michigan water to IL-14 so our communities can have a secure water source for generations to come.

Downtown Toronto does that. Downtown gets its water from the Toronto Island Water Treatment Plant which draws from deep out in the bottom of Lake Ontario, so the water is very cold. The treated water is then pumped around downtown to cool buildings while bringing the fresh drinking water to taps.
the line 5 pipeline is a ticking time bomb running under the great lakes and this is what she’s doing, fuck

The Great Lakes provide drinking water for millions, yet more than 22 million pounds of plastic waste enter them every year. 🚰 Protecting our water requires more than cleanup. We must prevent plastic pollution at the source by reducing plastic use and investing in safer solutions & reuse systems.

In 2014, the tap water in Toledo was unsafe to bathe in -- much less drink -- effectively poisoned by an overgrowth of toxic algae in Lake Erie. In response, the state started a drinking water protection program called H2Ohio. Now that program is facing deep cuts.

this is a storage tunnel, one of 7 such tunnels we are building (2011-2035) to control and eliminate combined sewer overflows discharging to Lake Erie. these tunnels are ~200 feet underground and they maximize system capacity to allow our treatment plants time to treat the flow during heavy storms.
Leave your water running. A thin stream out of the faucet(s). A slightly higher water bill beats busted pipes and flooding every time. Trust me. - A northerner on the Great Lakes.

The Great Lakes contain roughly 20% of the world's surface freshwater and
about 90% of the freshwater in the United States. This tunnel needs to stop and Line 5 needs to be shut down, we can’t take chances!!

No Line 5! It does not benefit Michigan, and it is a ticking time bomb that will destroy our Great Lakes! Why does Moolenaar continue to push for legislation that hurts his constituents?

The clock is ticking on Line 5 and critical safety questions remain unanswered. PHMSA has never preemptively shut down a pipeline, and waiting is a choice we can’t afford to make in the Great Lakes.

I will never pass an opportunity to remind my fellow Americans that tap water is just fine for drinking, and is in fact a triumph of our civilization. (If you have stinky well water, I'm sorry. I live in the Great Lakes watershed and we have fabulous tap water.)

I don't filter my water, either. Water in some parts of the country may taste funky, but by and large western civilization's greatest health triumph is our drinking water. (Look to RFKJr. to fuck it up any day now.) I am fortunate to live near the Great Lakes, where the H20 is 💯.

The statewide septic-code package, SB 299-300, would have required routine inspections and fixes to stop failing septics from leaking into rivers and the Great Lakes. It passed the Senate, then died in the House after a GOP walkout.
